name: Lower Navarrese Basque

ancestors.notes: Lower Navarrese inherits the standard Basque grapheme base and is typologically intermediate, sharing aspiration features with Souletin while retaining core Batua consonantism.

notes: Lower Navarrese (Behe-Nafar). Spoken in Basse-Navarre (Pyrénées-Atlantiques, France). In Zuazo's classification it forms, together with Lapurdian, the single Navarro-Lapurdian dialect; phonologically it is an eastern (continental) aspirating variety. VALIDATED against Hualde (2018), 'Aspiration in Basque'. Aspiration is preserved: the laryngeal fricative /h/ is a productive phoneme (Hualde 2018: 1-3), and voiceless stops have aspirated allophones/variants [pʰ tʰ kʰ] whose distribution is constrained to the onset of a stressed syllable within a two-syllable window near the start of the word, reflecting an older stress-conditioned aspiration (Michelena FHV, as reported in Hualde 2018: 5-6). These aspirated stops are less systematically phonemic than in Souletin, where they form a full three-way contrast (Hualde 2018: 2). The three-way sibilant contrast /s̻ s̺ ʃ/ of the base is retained. NOTE (engine limit): the exact stress-window restriction on aspirated stops is not expressed at the grapheme level; [pʰ tʰ kʰ] are listed as conditioned variants only.

sources.author: Hualde, J.I.
sources.title: Aspiration in Basque
sources.notes: Primary read source: preservation of /h/ (pp.1-3), stress-window distribution of aspirated stops (pp.5-6), contrast with the Souletin three-way system (p.2).

sources.author: Hualde, J.I. & Ortiz de Urbina, J. (eds.)
sources.title: A Grammar of Basque

sources.author: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I.
sources.title: The Sounds of the World's Languages
